subject: DevOps
title: Continuous Delivery vs Deployment
description: A quick win: understand Continuous Delivery vs Deployment.

hook.text: Continuous delivery and continuous deployment sound the same. They're not. One key difference separates them.

buildup.text: Continuous delivery means every commit is release-ready. A human decides when to push the button.

discovery.text: Continuous deployment removes the human. Every passing commit goes straight to production automatically.

twist.text: Most companies start with delivery. Deployment requires extreme confidence in your test suite.

climax.text: Netflix and GitHub practice continuous deployment. Most enterprises use delivery with manual gates.

punchline.text: Delivery: always ready. Deployment: always shipping.

quiz.question: What distinguishes continuous deployment from continuous delivery?
options: Deployment sends every passing commit to production automatically
